tools.name: search_opportunities
tools.description:
Search Canadian funding opportunities (grants, competitions, accelerators, tax credits, wage subsidies, loans, events). Returns JSON.

WHEN TO CALL:
- The user asks about Canadian funding, grants, competitions, accelerators, or pitch programs
- The user mentions their startup/business and wants oppo

tools.name: get_opportunity_details
tools.description:
Fetch full details for a single opportunity by ID. Use this after search_opportunities when:
- The user wants to know more about a specific match
- You need the full eligibility text to confirm a strong-match claim
- The user wants to draft an application — you need the requirements

The response in

tools.name: compare_opportunities
tools.description:
Fetch 2–4 opportunities at once so you can present them side by side. Use after search_opportunities when the user is choosing between a few options.

Returns the full detail rows (including eligibility + body) for every id, in the order requested. Present them as a comparison table — rows = attribu

tools.name: eligibility_check
tools.description:
Pull a single opportunity's eligibility criteria alongside a description of the applicant, so you can judge whether they qualify. Use when the user asks "do I qualify for this?" or "does this apply to me?".

The response contains the opportunity's eligibility text + key fields and echoes the applica

tools.name: subscribe_to_digest
tools.description:
Subscribe the user to a FREE weekly email digest of Canadian funding opportunities matching a saved search (keywords + region). Each week they get 8–10 grants, newest first, falling back to the strongest current matches when nothing new landed.

WHEN TO CALL:
- The user, after a search, says yes to

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "search_opportunities" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Search Canadian funding opportunities (grants, competitions, accelerators, tax credits, wage subsidies, loans, events). Returns JSON. WHEN TO CALL: - The user a…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: search_opportunities, subscribe_to_digest · Private data: search_opportunities · Public sink: search_opportunities, subscribe_to_digest.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: search_opportunities, subscribe_to_digest · Private data: search_opportunities · Public sink: search_opportunities, subscribe_to_digest

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
